🏞️

Day 1: Splash & Thrills!

📍 24 May · Lost World of Tambun, Ipoh
First stop — Lost World of Tambun! 🏞️✌️

The adventure kicked off at Lost World of Tambun — a water and theme park nestled among limestone hills! The family cooled off in the water park, braved the thrilling slides, and maybe even met some friendly animals at the petting zoo!

After a full day of fun, they checked into Onsen Premium Suites Tambun by BYON (RM 265.57) — the perfect place to soak and relax after a splashing day! 🌊

💡 Fun Fact! Lost World of Tambun sits on a natural hot spring — that's why they have an onsen too! ♨️
🧸

Day 2: Lucky at the Claws!

📍 25 May · Genting Highlands
Look at those prizes! All three girls won! 🧸🎉

The family checked into Resorts World Genting — SkyWorlds Hotel (RM 472.25). First stop: Space Panda Plus for claw machines! And boy, was everyone lucky today!

Yu Tong snagged 1 plushie 🧸
Yu Tian grabbed 2! 🧸🧸
Little Yu Jia also got 2! 🧸🧸

5 prizes in one session! The girls were on fire! 🔥

Later, they headed to Genting Bowl for some bowling fun (RM 72)! The staff was super helpful — they gave the kids a special ramp to help roll the ball down the lane. Everyone had a blast! 🎳

💡 Fun Fact! Claw machines are called "UFO Catchers" in Japan! 🛸
🎢

Day 3: Theme Park & Last Night

📍 26 May · Genting Highlands
At the entrance of Genting SkyWorlds! 🎢🎉

The big day at Genting Theme Park (RM 417)! Yu Tong and Yu Tian's biggest challenge was the roller coaster 🎢 — and they were brave enough to ride it! Yu Jia cheered them on from below! Their absolute favourite was the 360° spinning chair — all three girls couldn't get enough! 🔄

At night, they tried the claw machines again at Space Panda Plus. But this time, luck wasn't on their side... They spent RM 50 and only won on the very last token! Everyone screamed with joy! After that consolation prize, they vowed never to challenge the claw machine again! 😅

Since it was the last night at Genting, everyone dragged their tired bodies out to find bubble tea. Sadly, their favourite milk tea shops had all closed down. After walking far, they settled at Starbucks — the cold mountain breeze made it a cozy end to the night! ☕🌬️

💡 Fun Fact! The glass floor is VERY high up! But everyone was brave for the photo!
🚡

Day 4: Cable Car & Shopping

📍 27 May · Genting Premium Outlet → Ipoh
Scenic overlook with cable cars behind! 🚡🏙️

After breakfast, the kids got to ride the cable car 🚡 — it went all the way down to the mid-hill Genting Premium Outlet! Mama enjoyed shopping 🛍️ while the kids played at the playground. They had Baskin Robbins ice cream 🍦 and finally got their bubble tea — fulfilling yesterday's dream! 🧋

Time flew by until evening. Papa took the cable car back up to get the car, drove down the hill to pick up everyone. After a 4-hour drive 🚗, they arrived in Ipoh, exhausted but happy.

That night, they dragged their tired bodies to a self-service laundry 🧺, then packed dinner from the famous Tuck Kee Restaurant 🍜 — the perfect ending to a long day!

They spent the night at Anderson Residences HomeStay (RM 138.81).

💡 Fun Fact! Genting Premium Outlet is the only outlet mall in Malaysia perched on a mountainside!
🕳️

Day 5: The Big Cave!

📍 28 May · Gua Tempurung, Ipoh
The whole crew at the cave entrance! 🕳️✌️

Gua Tempurung is a HUGE cave — one of the longest in Malaysia at 4km long! The whole family went inside to see amazing rock formations, stalactites hanging from the ceiling, and maybe even bats!

Entry was only RM 42 for the family — a bargain for an epic adventure! Yu Tian wore a special lanyard and led the way like a real tour guide! 🧭 Yu Tong helped carry things and looked after her little sisters, while Yu Jia was the bravest little spelunker, fascinated by every stalactite! Everyone felt like real explorers!

After the cave, they headed to Ah Jie Restaurant for a well-deserved meal! 🥟

🌟 Evening Bonus! At 6pm, the family headed back to Lost World of Tambun — they have an annual pass, so why not make it worth it! They spent a lovely evening with the animals, and Papa's favourite was getting to pet the capybara! 🐹 After that, they soaked in the hot springs ♨️ — the perfect way to unwind.

Before heading back, they stopped by Bila Bila convenience store 🛍️ for some shopping, then returned to Anderson Residences HomeStay (RM 138.81) for another cosy night.

💡 Fun Fact! "Gua Tempurung" means "Big Drum Cave" in Malay!
🌸

Day 6: Trekking with Ah Tian!

📍 29 May · Cameron Highlands Jungle
At Taman Konservasi Rafflesia with Ah Tian's truck! 🚚🌸

At 10am, the family headed into the jungle led by Ah Tian 🧗 — a super famous mountain guide! They sat at the back of his truck alongside a bunch of Chinese tourists. Ah Tian is well-known on Xiaohongshu (RED) 📕, and the whole car only had their family of 5 as Malaysians!

They were super lucky — they spotted 3 Rafflesia flowers 🌸🌸🌸 and even a beautiful waterfall 💧! The Rafflesia can grow up to 1 meter wide and only blooms for a few days each year. Yu Tong couldn't stop taking photos, Yu Tian was fascinated by every leaf and creature, and little Yu Jia kept pointing and gasping at every flower! Three in one trip was incredible!

By 3pm, they came out of the mountain and headed to Palas Horizon Residence HomeStay (RM 304.72) for self check-in. After settling down, they went to find dinner — finally grabbing a late meal at Cameron Macdonalds around 10pm! 🍔

🤢 Smelly Fact! Rafflesia smells like rotten meat to attract flies! Pee-yew!
🎂

Day 7: Papa's Birthday!

📍 30 May · Cameron Highlands
Mama & the three girls among the tea leaves 🍵✌️

On the last full day, the family had breakfast at Old Town White Coffee ☕, then checked out of Palas Horizon. The destination was Westwood Highland, but the traffic was huge 🚗💨, so they decided to stop by a tea farm (RM 8) — and it turned into a beautiful photo session! The green tea bushes stretched on forever against the cool mountain air. 📸🍵

After a rest, they continued to Westwood Highland HomeStay (RM 333.84), located between Kampung Raja and Kuala Terla right in the middle of a flower farm 🌸. It's a cozy, warm hostel with clean rooms, free WiFi, a fully equipped kitchen, a garden-view dining area, and even a BBQ pit!

And the best part? That night, the family celebrated Papa's birthday! 🎉🎂 A perfect way to end an incredible trip!

💡 Fun Fact! Cameron Highlands is 1,500m above sea level — that's why it's so nice and cool! 🥶
🏠

Day 8: Heading Home

📍 31 May · Cameron → Home
Last morning at Westwood Highland before heading home! 🏠❤️

Time to say goodbye! Before heading back, the family hung around Westwood Highland and walked to a nearby wholesale flower market 🌸 — the flowers were incredibly cheap! Mama probably couldn't resist buying a huge bouquet! 💐

For lunch, they stopped at a nearby restaurant 维八 (Wei Ba) 🍚. The kids tried economy rice for the first time — they thought it was a buffet! They loaded up their plates and absolutely loved it! 😂🍛

After a wonderful meal, they went straight back home. 8 days, 5 people, a lifetime of memories. 🏔️❤️

💡 Fun Fact! Cameron Highlands supplies most of Malaysia's flowers, vegetables, and tea! That's why the wholesale flowers are so cheap!
💰

🏆 8 days, 5 people, countless memories

🌟 Fun Facts! 🌟

🏔️
Cameron is 1,500m high!
That's like 500 houses stacked on top of each other!
🌸
Rafflesia is the biggest flower!
It can be 1 meter wide — bigger than a bike wheel!
🕳️
Gua Tempurung is 4km long!
Imagine walking 4km inside a dark cave!
🚡
Genting has cable cars!
The cable car goes up a mountain — so high!
🍵
Tea comes from leaves!
The tea we drink comes from those green bushes!
👨‍👩‍👧‍👧
8 days of family fun!
The best holiday ever with Mama, Papa, and sisters!
🏔️

THE END

What a fun trip!
